# Notation Standards and Conventions {#notation-standards}

This chapter defines all notation, terminology, and standards used throughout this pattern making documentation.

## Measurement System

All measurements in this book use the **metric system** (centimeters).

| Unit | Abbreviation | Usage |
|------|-------------|-------|
| Centimeter | cm | All body measurements, pattern dimensions |
| Millimeter | mm | Small details (notches, seam allowance) |
| Meter | m | Fabric lengths only |

**Important**: Never mix imperial and metric. All formulas assume centimeter inputs.

## Coordinate System

Patterns are drafted on a 2D Cartesian coordinate system:

- **Origin (0,0)**: Bottom-left corner of the foundation rectangle
- **X-axis**: Horizontal, positive to the right (pattern width)
- **Y-axis**: Vertical, positive upward (pattern height/length)

\vspace{1cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.6\textwidth]{images/diagrams/coordinate_system.pdf}
\end{center}
\vspace{1cm}

## Point Naming Conventions

Construction points follow the traditional pattern drafting convention:

- **Uppercase letters** (A, B, C...): Primary construction points
- **Sequential order**: Points are named in order of construction
- **Letter meaning**: Some letters have traditional meanings:
  - A-D: Foundation rectangle corners
  - E-F: Side seam offset
  - G-H: Center division (armscye line)
  - I-L: Front piece divisions
  - M-P: Shoulder and neckline points
  - Q-U: Armscye curve control points
  - V-Z: Additional curve control points

## Line Types and Meanings

Based on the [FreeSewing Notation System](https://freesewing.eu/docs/about/notation/):

```{r line-types-demo, fig.cap="Line types used in pattern plots", fig.height=6, fig.width=8}
# Create demonstration plot of all line types
demo_plot <- create_pattern_plot(width = 20, height = 25, 
                                 title = "Line Type Reference")

# Add examples of each line type
y_pos <- 24
for (type in names(FREESEWING_LINETYPES)) {
  demo_plot <- demo_plot +
    annotate("segment", x = 1, xend = 19, y = y_pos, yend = y_pos,
             color = FREESEWING_COLORS[[type]],
             linetype = FREESEWING_LINETYPES[[type]],
             linewidth = 0.8) +
    annotate("text", x = 10, y = y_pos + 0.3,
             label = paste(type, "-", FREESEWING_LINETYPES[[type]]),
             size = 3)
  y_pos <- y_pos - 1
}
demo_plot
```

Line Type	Appearance	Meaning
Seam	Solid thick line	Sewing line
Seam allowance	Dashed line	Edge of fabric
Grainline	Dotted with arrow	Fabric grain direction
Cut-on-fold	Dash-dot with arrows	Place on fabric fold
Dimension	Thin dotted	Measurement reference
Note	Dashed thin	Construction notes
Mark	Solid thin	Placement marks
Contrast	Solid colored	Highlight specific areas
Help	Dotted thin	Construction guides
Notches and Markings
Notch Types
{r

notch_plot <- create_pattern_plot(width = 10, height = 5, title = "Notch Types")
notch_plot <- add_notch(notch_plot, 3, 2.5, type = "front", size = 0.3)
notch_plot <- add_notch(notch_plot, 7, 2.5, type = "back", size = 0.3)
notch_plot

    Front notch (⊙): Indicates front of garment

    Back notch (⊗): Indicates back of garment

Other Markings
Symbol	Meaning
⊙	Front notch
⊗	Back notch
⊕	Button
⊘	Buttonhole
─●─	Bartack
⇅	Grainline
⇶	Cut on fold
Pattern Piece Information

Every pattern piece includes:

    Piece name and number (e.g., "1 - Front Bodice")

    Size identifier

    Cut quantity (e.g., "Cut 2 fabric")

    Date of drafting

    Scale box (5cm × 5cm verification square)

Color Standards
{r

color_plot <- create_pattern_plot(width = 20, height = 8, title = "Fabric Type Colors")
colors_to_show <- c("fabric_primary", "fabric_lining", "fabric_interfacing")
x_pos <- 2
for (col_name in colors_to_show) {
  color_plot <- color_plot +
    annotate("rect", xmin = x_pos, xmax = x_pos + 4, 
             ymin = 3, ymax = 5,
             fill = FREESEWING_COLORS[[col_name]]) +
    annotate("text", x = x_pos + 2, y = 6,
             label = gsub("_", " ", col_name),
             size = 3)
  x_pos <- x_pos + 6
}
color_plot

Color	Fabric Type
Black (#212121)	Main fabric
Blue (#1976D2)	Lining
Orange (#F57C00)	Interfacing
Grid System

All pattern plots include:

    Major grid: Every 5 cm (solid light grey lines)

    Minor grid: Every 1 cm (very light dotted lines)

    Purpose: Easy visual measurement and scale verification

Printing Conventions

For full-scale printing:

    Test square: Always print a 5cm × 5cm test square first

    Scale: 1:1 (100%) unless otherwise noted

    Paper size: A0 for plotter, A4/Letter tiled for home printing

    Registration marks: Cross marks (+) at tile corners for alignment

File Naming Conventions
Pattern Type	File Format	Example
Rendered plots	PDF	bodice_front_v1.pdf
Full-scale patterns	PDF	bodice_size48_A0.pdf
Tiled patterns	PDF	bodice_size48_A4_tiled.pdf
Measurement files	CSV	female_size48.csv
Ease parameters	CSV	bodice_ease_female.csv
Version Control

All patterns follow semantic versioning:

    Major version (v1, v2): Significant design changes

    Minor version (v1.1, v1.2): Small adjustments

    Patch version (v1.1.1): Error corrections

Example: bodice_front_female_v2.1.3_size48.pdf
